title: milium vulgare var. verticillata
intro: Milium vulgare var. verticillata, also known as milium or wild garlic, is a perennial plant belonging to the lily family (Liliaceae). Traditionally, milium has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of milium include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Milium can be prepared as an herbal tea, oil, or tincture, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of conditions, from skin irritations to digestive issues.
